thought.text: Simple chain merges fine — entry → a → b → c → ret all merged. Let me try with a PHI in the destination to check that case:

action.command:
cd /workspace/YatCC && cat > /tmp/test2.ll << 'EOF'
define i32 @main(i32 %x) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %x, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %a, label %b
a:
  br label %merge
b:
  br label %merge
merge:
  %phi = phi i32 [ 1, %a ], [ 2, %b ]
  ret i32 %phi
}
EOF
./build/task/4/task4-classic /tmp/test2.ll /tmp/test2_out.ll 2>&1 | tail -5 ; echo "EXIT $?"; cat /tmp/test2_out.ll

tool_call.name: terminal
tool_call.arguments:
{"command": "cd /workspace/YatCC && cat > /tmp/test2.ll << 'EOF'
define i32 @main(i32 %x) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %x, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %a, label %b
a:
  br label %merge
b:
  br label %merge
merge:
  %phi = phi i32 [ 1, %a ], [ 2, %b ]
  ret i32 %phi
}
EOF
./build/task/4/task4-classic /tmp/test2.ll /tmp/test2_out.ll 2>&1 | tail -5 ; echo "EXIT $?"; cat /tmp/test2_out.ll", "security_risk": "LOW", "summary": "Test PHI merging"}
